message.content: {"findings":[{"capability_id":"UPLOAD_PATH_ISOLATION","issue":"Uploaded files are saved under a public uploads directory using the user-supplied filename, which can allow path traversal or unsafe placement of attacker-controlled content in a public root.","evidence_span":"app.py: UPLOAD_ROOT = Path(\"/public/uploads\"); upload(filename: str, content_type: str, file_bytes: bytes): if filename.endswith((\".png\", \".jpg\")): save(str(UPLOAD_ROOT / filename), file_bytes); config.yaml: upload_storage: /public/uploads","recommended_fix":"Generate server-side random filenames, normalize and reject path components in user input, and store uploads outside public executable/static roots. Serve files through controlled application endpoints with authorization and safe content handling."}]}
